name: Ojibwa

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Ojibwa (None).

notes: Skeleton. Algic (Algonquian) language of the Great Lakes written in the Fiero double-vowel Roman orthography. Long vowels are written as doubled letters (aa ii oo) plus e=[eː]; short a=[ə], i=[ɪ]. The lenis/fortis stop contrast is spelled b/p, d/t, g/k and mapped as voiced vs voiceless; fortis length and predictable nasalization (nh) are left unencoded, raising the PER floor.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Ojibwa (None)
